title: Add Change Column to every time period automation

posts.author: jpendleton (Joann Pendleton)
posts.cookedHtml:
Description

Add the ability to set a date using every time period.

What are you trying to achieve

I want to send a notification on the 1st of every month to specific users when a column is empty. There is no easy way to set a date every month without it having been manually entered. If I can set the reminder date every month to a specific day of the month then this would be possible.

posts.author: YOGESHP (Yogesh Pokale)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @jpendleton, this is possible with this automation setup.

Using the single automation “When Date arrives → notify user and push Date by 1 month”, you can:

  • Send a notification on a specific day (for example, the 1st of every month), and

  • Automatically move the date forward by one month, creating a recurring monthly reminder.

    image1708×203 24.6 KB

This removes the need to manually re-enter the date each month and achieves the same outcome as a recurring monthly schedule.

Hope this helps!

posts.author: WirkKarl (Karl)
posts.cookedHtml: Totally agree being able to set a recurring date every month would make automations like this much easier. Right now, most people end up manually updating the date or using workarounds with formulas.

posts.author: jpendleton (Joann Pendleton)
posts.cookedHtml:
It is really hard going from Smartsheet to Monday. You can’t automate based on a formula column.

Auto-pilot hub should have been able to handle this, but When a Date Arrives only triggers for one row.

We are HIPAA and can’t use outside apps….

posts.author: jpendleton (Joann Pendleton)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes, but I still need to be able to set the date to the 1st of the month when a new item is added. The loop can’t be closed to ensure someone doesn’t forget to set the date….

I did add a notification to myself when a new item is added, but it is not a very good work-around if I’m not around or miss it or even leave the company.

posts.author: YOGESHP (Yogesh Pokale)
posts.cookedHtml:
That makes total sense. Having a process that depends on someone remembering to set a date (or catching a notification) isn’t reliable long term, especially if you’re unavailable.

If you’re open to third-party apps, this is one area where low-code platforms like Make or Zapier can help.

posts.author: jpendleton (Joann Pendleton)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks, we are talking to Make, but we need it to be HIPAA for Healthcare.
